loss_record.structural_divergence: None is a named constant (singleton object), not a type constructor; NoneType is not user-definable or instantiable; Python conflates the unit type with its unique value; no explicit 'Unit type' syntax distinct from the value None
loss_record.domain_narrowing: None is overloaded as both unit value and null/absent sentinel; unit use and null-sentinel use are syntactically indistinguishable; realization domain narrowed to programs where None is used in the unit role only; mixing both uses violates unit_singleton contract
loss_record.ub_introduction: none: Python has no undefined behavior; None is a safe, well-defined singleton; no memory corruption or undefined state
loss_record.effect_divergence: none: None carries no effects; returning None is side-effect-free with respect to the unit contract
loss_record.value_divergence: none: None is the unique inhabitant of NoneType, satisfying unit_singleton(None); all values of NoneType are provably equal; no value-representation gap
